title: dracocephalum imberbe var. laniflorum
intro: Dracocephalum imberbe var. laniflorum, also known as the woolly dragonhead or laniflorum dragonhead, is a variety of flowering plant in the mint family, characterized by its hairy leaves and purple flowers. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and protect the skin from infections and wounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and promoting digestion. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Dracocephalum imberbe var. laniflorum can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
